definition: A field or domain of experience, especially in analytical contexts involving the sense fields and their objects.
notes: The repo now uses field as the broad default for āyatana because it scales better across compounds than cognitive realm and avoids prematurely narrowing every case to a sense organ or abstract metaphysical base. Sense field remains the preferred alternate when the sixfold experiential structure needs to be explicit. Base can still work in established compounds, but it should not dominate the lexicon when field would read more naturally in contemporary English. Translators should keep the family stable between āyatana and saḷāyatana and should not let base silently replace field except where a local compound or formula clearly requires it.

context_rules.context: In general analytical contexts where āyatana names a domain of experience
In the sixfold experiential system or where the sensory function needs to be explicit
context_rules.notes: Use when the relation to eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind is the point.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing scope or range rather than sensory function
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate when it reads more clearly.

context_rules.context: In compounds where conventional Buddhist-English phrasing is unusually entrenched
context_rules.notes: Use sparingly and only when field would be clumsy.

example_phrases.notes: Illustrates compound handling under the house default.
Useful where dhamma functions as the object-side field of mind.

version_notes: Changed the default from cognitive realm to field and added explicit rules for sense-field, domain, and base usage.
